title: 3M (MMM)Intrinsic Value
description: A transparent, step-by-step discounted cash flow valuation of 3M Company (MMM). Every assumption disclosed. Here's what 3M is really worth.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale:
" data-astro-cid-p4o3gzuv>
Conservative outlook. 3M has historically struggled to achieve high single-digit top-line growth. Post-Solventum spin-off and after settling major litigation, a 4% CAGR reflects stabilization and optimization rather than aggressive expansion.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale:
" data-astro-cid-p4o3gzuv>
A mature industrial company should grow roughly in line with inflation or slightly below global GDP growth. 2.5% assumes 3M maintains its economic moat in materials science but does not outpace general economic expansion.

verdictFooter: Based on FY2025 actuals. Data via S&P Global (Mar 18, 2026).

items.title: Litigation Payments
items.description: 3M has agreed to pay billions over the next decade for the Combat Arms Earplugs ($6B) and PFAS public water systems ($10.3B) settlements. While structured, these will act as a significant drag on actual free cash flow available to shareholders.

items.title: Solventum Spin-off
items.description: The spin-off of its healthcare unit (Solventum) removed a historically stable, higher-margin revenue stream. 3M is now more exposed to cyclical industrial end-markets.

items.title: Dividend Cut
items.description: Following the Solventum spin-off, 3M reset its dividend payout to reflect the new, smaller company structure, breaking its decades-long streak as a Dividend Aristocrat. This changes the investor base profile.

items.title: Macroeconomic Sensitivity
items.description: As an industrial conglomerate, 3M's revenue is highly sensitive to global manufacturing output, consumer electronics demand, and automotive production.
